RIC-30; RSC-16a. "Tribute Penny" type. Obverse: Laureate head right; Reverse: Livia, as Pax, seated right on chair, holding scepter and olive branch, and with feet on footstool; ornate chair legs, single line below.

From the John P. Burnham Collection.

Estimate: 300 - 500...

type_info.label: Tiberius AR Denarius — Tribute Penny
type_info.notes: Actually Tiberius, not Augustus — but known universally as the "Tribute Penny" from the New Testament (Matthew 22:21). Obv: Tiberius right. Rev: Livia (as Pax) seated right. The most collected Roman coin by Christians worldwide. Enormous demand drives premium regardless of condition. Even Fine examples sell well. Grade matters less than for other types due to sentimental collector base.
